channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Is anything collectible worth collecting? What experts say
item.description: You may have been promised as a child that collecting plush toys and waxy baseball cards would pay off in the future.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration argues judge can't order return of man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge didn't have the authority to order the Trump administration to broker the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported from the U.S. to a notorious El Salvador prison, government attorneys argued Saturday as they urged an appeals court to suspend the ruling.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from Trump's move to send Venezuelan migrants in the US to a prison in El Salvador
item.description: On Friday, March 14, President Donald Trump invoked the Alien Enemies Act, a 1798 law giving him immense powers to deport noncitizens in a time of war.
item.dc:creator: TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump goes all in with bet that the heavy price of tariffs will pay off for Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Not even 24 hours after his party lost a key Wisconsin race and underperformed in Florida, President Donald Trump followed the playbook that has defined his political career: He doubled down. Trump’s move on Wednesday to place stiff new tariffs on imports from nearly all U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yoon Suk Yeol removed as South Korea's president over short-lived martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s Constitutional Court unanimously removed Yoon Suk Yeol from office Friday, ending his tumultuous presidency and setting up a new election, four months after he threw the nation into turmoil with an ill-fated declaration of martial law.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Georgia bill would compensate the wrongfully convicted and let Trump recover costs of election case
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A revived attempt to fix Georgia's inefficient system for compensating people wrongfully convicted of crimes almost died.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justice Department seeks 7 year sentence for George Santos
item.description: Prosecutors said the length of the requested sentence — 87 months — reflects the serious nature of Santos’s “unparalleled” crimes.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shopping spree? Consumers rush to buy goods ahead of Trump tariffs
item.description: President Trump's tariffs are expected to drive up prices, and some consumers are trying to get ahead of the action.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KTLA 5

item.title: Las Vegas Sphere launching 'experience' around classic film
item.description: Sphere, the famed immersive Las Vegas entertainment arena, is set to debut two new "Sphere Experiences" later this year — and one is based on a beloved classic motion picture.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors seek 7 years in prison for disgraced ex-US Rep. George Santos in federal fraud case
item.description: Prosecutors are seeking more than seven years in prison for disgraced former congressman George Santos after he pleaded guilty to federal fraud and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Farmers fear tariffs could cost them one of their biggest markets in China
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Heading into this year, most U.S. farmers were hoping to break even or maybe record a small profit if they could find a way to limit their sky-high costs.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: JP Morgan predicts 2025 recession as Trump tariffs rattle market
item.description: President Trump's tariffs and their fallout will cause the U.S. economy to enter a recession this year, J.P. Morgan predicts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Anaheim mayor wants Los Angeles Angels to change their name
item.description: In an open-letter about the Angels future, the Anaheim mayor called for the team to be named for the city it actually represents.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Energy Department considers more than 40 percent of its staff non-essential as layoffs loom
item.description: The Energy Department's portfolio includes both energy technology and innovation as well as nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Institutional investors, uber-rich have seen trillions erased in the markets -- for now
item.description: President Donald Trump's trade war has seen trillions of dollars in wealth tied to the global markets evaporate within days. While all investors are feeling the pressure, experts say the wealthiest Americans are seeing perhaps the biggest impact.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration issues demands on Harvard as conditions for billions in federal money
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has issued a list of demands Harvard University must meet as a condition for receiving almost $9 billion in grants and contracts, federal money that is being threatened during an investigation into campus antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Trump admin's bid to move Tufts student case to Louisiana; sends it Vermont
item.description: A federal judge on Friday denied a Trump administration request to move a case involving a detained Tufts University doctoral student to Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal officials are quietly terminating the legal residency of some international college students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A crackdown on foreign students is alarming college leaders, who say the Trump administration is using new tactics and vague justifications to push some students out of the country. College officials worry the new approach will keep foreigners from wanting to study in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, ANNIE MA and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Authorities identify father, 2 sons killed by Pennsylvania Amtrak train
item.description: Officials on Friday identified the three family members who were fatally hit by an Amtrak train near Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, along with their manners of death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to give TikTok another 75-day extension
item.description: President Trump said Friday that he plans to give TikTok another 75-day extension as his administration nears a deal to avert a ban on the popular social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: TSA officers find prohibited item in traveler's flip-flop at DC airport
item.description: TSA officers working at Washington Dulles on March 30 were able to stop a traveler from bringing the item through security, a spokesperson for the agency said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tornadoes will become more frequent in coming weeks. Here's what to know if your home is destroyed
item.description: An average of 1,200 tornadoes hit the United States yearly, and the country will probably get more killer supercells spawning tornadoes and hail as the world warms, according to a 2023 study.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Viral video shows Florida charter captain arguing with boater; police investigating
item.description: A viral video showing a fight between two Florida boaters has sparked both a police investigation and strong reactions from people online.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RealPage sues California city officials over rental algorithm ban
item.description: Real estate software company RealPage filed a federal lawsuit Wednesday against Berkeley, California — the latest city to try to block landlords from using algorithms when deciding rents. Officials in many cities claim the practice is anti-competitive and is driving up the price of housing.
item.dc:creator: R.J. RICO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Remains of Illinois woman who disappeared in 1983 found after cold case investigators start podcast
item.description: Karen Schepers' remains were finally identified after being found in a car submerged in the Fox River, nearly 43 years after she went missing.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, Fox 5

item.title: Tornadoes, winds, flooding and hail: Arkansas wakes up to disastrous severe storm damage
item.description: Communities across Arkansas are waking up to severe storm damage as the state continues to prepare for more potential severe weather later this week.
item.dc:creator: Cate Skinner, NewsNation

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could significantly impact California’s exports, such as automobiles, computer equipment and semiconductors, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump: Tariff rollout 'going very well,' says markets will 'boom' amid slump
item.description: President Trump on Thursday insisted that the rollout of his new reciprocal tariffs is "going really well" despite markets taking a plunge and foreign leaders appearing rattled by the prospect of a global recession.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: White House postpones Saturday garden tours due to anti-Trump demonstrations
item.description: The White House is rescheduling one of its annual spring garden tour dates due to a "massive" anti-Trump demonstration expected to take place near 1600 Pennsylvania Ave.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, ABC 4

item.title: White House nears deadline to avoid TikTok ban, potential deal in the works
item.description: The Trump administration is racing against the clock to secure a deal that would keep TikTok operational in the United States ahead of Saturday’s deadline.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, WGN 9

item.title: Deadly storms slash through the South and Midwest
item.description: The coming days were also forecast to bring the risk of potentially deadly flash flooding to the South and Midwest as severe thunderstorms blowing eastward become supercharged.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: White House defends not including Russia, North Korea on tariffs
item.description: The White House on Thursday defended its decision to not include Russia, North Korea, Cuba or Belarus in the latest round of tariffs, which targeted dozens of global trading partners that were labeled the “worst offenders” when it came to trade barriers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What Trump's trade war means for American families
item.description: Stocks are down sharply as Wall Street firms recalibrate their economic growth estimates amid President Trump's trade war. Economists predict the new tariffs could lead to increased consumer prices and inflation, potentially costing American households between $3,000 to $5,000 more per year.
item.dc:creator: David Lazarus, KTLA 5

item.title: Yankees OF Brett Gardner's son died of carbon monoxide poisoning, had 'layer' on organs, officials say
item.description: Randall Zúñiga, director of the Judicial Investigation Agency, said they tested the 14-year-old Miller Gardner for carboxyhemoglobin, a compound generated when carbon monoxide binds to hemoglobin in the blood.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Girl, 7, struck, killed by falling boulder at ski resort
item.description: A 7-year-old girl was killed by a falling boulder at a popular ski resort in Lake Tahoe over the weekend, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dow drops 1,600 as US stocks lead worldwide sell-off after Trump’s tariffs cause a COVID-like shock
item.description: Financial markets around the world are reeling Thursday following President Donald Trump’s latest and most severe set of tariffs, and the U.S. stock market is taking the worst of it so far.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Juice sold from New York to wineries in 12 states recalled over potentially deadly toxin
item.description: A wine juice wholesaler in New York has recalled a pumpkin wine juice sold to wineries in a dozen states because it contains a toxin that could be deadly.
item.dc:creator: Madalyn Buursma, Fox 5
